entry.title: Access iOS native platform APIs from SAP Mobile Development kit project
entry.content:
• With any SAP MDK (Mobile Development Kit) based iOS projects, including the SSAM (SAP Service and Asset Manager) projects, often, we get a requirement that can be solved within minutes if only we have access to the native iOS apis.
  • In this blog, I will explain how to leverage native apis easily from your MDK Project.

  • Firstly, we have to have our swift class file ready. This file should have the logic to access the platform api that is needed. For example, The following class is written to retrieve the path of the iOS shared container. 

In this case, we are accessing the FileManager native api to retrieve the secured path of the shared container.

Step 1:

  • Navigate to your .mdkproject directory and get in to 'App_Resources/iOS/' directory and create a new directory named 'src' and paste the native class file here.

Step 2:

  • Use this .mdkproject directory to create your MDK Client.

Step 3:

  • Open a terminal on the root directory of the MDK Client that you have created and run the following command

 

ns typings ios

 

Step 4:

  • After this, from the root directory of the MDK client, navigate to 'typings/ios/arm64/'. Here, you will find 'objc!nsswiftsupport.d.ts' file.

Step 5:

  • Copy 'objc!nsswiftsupport.d.ts' file and paste it in the root directory of the MDK client as shown below.

Step 6:

  • In the MDK client root directory, open 'references.d.ts' file and add an entry for your file 'objc!nsswiftsupport.d.ts' as shown below.

Step 7:

  • That's it, we are all set. Now you can simply instantiate your native class in your MDK js rule and access all its functions and variables. You can safely ignore the reference error that is highlighted by your editor.

 

const sharedContainerpath = sharedContainerURL.new()
  let sharedContainerPath = sharedContainerpath.sharedContainerPathWithAppGroup('group.test')

 

Note: Please verify the variable/function names in the 'objc!nsswiftsupport.d.ts' file. As, it tends to get changed slightly compared to what you have actually written in your swift class file. You need to use the variable/function names mentioned in this file when you are referencing it in your MDK .js rules.

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Removing leading zeroes using XPATH in Content Modifier
entry.content:
Hi @ZTK196 ,

You can try with: string(number(//number))

Hope it helps!

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Site Address Details Missing for location data source
entry.content:
Which is the data source where a location wise- site information with site address details can be found?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Site Address Details Missing for location data source
entry.content:
Dear All,

Currently, the data source "SCM_LOCATION" does not contain address details. There is no standard report available to display address details based on location. 

Address details can be obtained through BO LOCATION, however there is no existing data source from SCM side that can retrieve address details based on location for display on reports. 

Kindly post this requirement via the Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence 

 

Best Regards,
Kopal Upadhyay

SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: TRM payment reference in Transaction Manager
entry.content: Hi Henrike, Thank you for your answer and document regarding payment reference in payment media. This document is covering the issue when the payment reference is not transferred to payment media, the issue we are facing is that field Payment reference is not available in TRM transaction where it should be entered. Kind regards, Slađana

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
I have now created a blog that explains this: Convert DMS Folder ID to Name in Automation

Enjoy 😺

entry.title: Business Rule to send a specific Checklist to an email address
entry.content:
Hi team,

Technicians have the possibility to fill specific Checkout/Checklist built in FSM.

Checklist are manually attached to the activity, at assignment and then closed and filled by FSEs.

Then they can be send under PDF format or Excel format from their device via the option "Print & Send".

Is there a possibility to create a business rule that could automatically send a specific checklist to an email address ? (under XLS format, for instance).

Regards

Brice

entry.title: View all material components for project - CN52N?
entry.content:
Hi there,

I'd like a way to view all, as in ALL, components for a project definition.

I've managed to get more or less the overview i need, using CN52N. For some materials, pegged requirements are displayed too, but for other header materials, no components are shown on the list.

Are there any tables that would be an idea to try?

Any idea why CN52N doesn't show all components?

 

BR,
Jonas J

entry.title: Re: TRM payment reference in Transaction Manager
entry.content: Hi Owen, thank you for your answer. Is there any information when this will be available for Public Cloud? As per current configuration, the option of creating payment request from TRM is not applicable as all relevant data cannot be entered and transferred to payment media making this option inappropriate for automatic payments. Kind regards, Slađana

entry.title: Mobile Start-Push Notification
entry.content:
Hi,

We want to connect our On-prem system with Mobile Start. After all configuration/assignment, we can see documents or pending list on Mobile Start, but we couldn't see/get push notification on our mobile devices. I will put steps we've done on the system. Can you guide us on what's missing?
For example, when an order is created, the goal is to display a notification on Mobile Start, as shown
in the images.

Completed Sections:
BTP Build Workzone has been enabled for mobile start notifications. The notification settings have
been activated from the site directory settings.

Notifications have been activated in the settings section.The Cloud Connector structure is as follows.

The S/4HANA system has been connected to the BTP system via Cloud Connector and Destination.A parameter has been added to the Destination Parameter section.

NOTIF_SERVICEPATH = /sap/opu/odata4/iwngw/notification/default/iwngw/notification_srv/0001

An RFC destination has been created for the SAP BTP notification. The RFC was set up using T-Code
SM59.Certificates have been added.

The SAP S/4HANA administrator accessed T-Code SE80 to create an OAuth 2.0 client profile.The SAP_CLOUD channel has been added to the notification channel using T-Code SPRO.

The IWNGW_NOTIF_CLOUD_BGRFC queue instance has been added to the bgRFC section.
The Notification section has been added to the SFF Notification configuration.

For testing, when a notification was sent via SAP GUI, the test notification was received in the Fiori
Launchpad, but no notification was received through BTP Build Workzone.

BTP Notification Screen
The configuration for receiving notifications when SAP standard processes occur, such as the
creation of a purchase order, has been completed. The steps are listed below:
PFCG role templates have been created. The Z_PUSH_NOTIFICATION single role has been created.
The SICF node has been activated. The Service Path field has been set to "/sap/opu/odata4".
The Notification OData Service has been published. The OData V4 service
/IWNGW/NOTIFICATION_SRV has been created.

 

The Push Channels have been configured. An ABAP Push Channel is used to send notifications to the
Fiori Launchpad. The recommended approach is to use a WebSocket connection for this purpose, as
WebSockets establish a bidirectional communication channel between the server and the client. The
WebSocket setup has been completed.
The Define Inbound Destination configuration has been checked.
The notification provider sections have been saved and activated.


New records have been created in the Notification section using T-Code SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1.

Example Notification

A connection has been established between Fiori Launchpad and BTP Workzone. Notifications for
the incoming orders appear in the My Inbox section of the Fiori Launchpad, but no notifications are
being received in BTP Workzone, even though the orders are visible in the My Inbox there. Due to
the inability to receive notifications, the Task Center has been set up.
Task Center Setup Procedures:
The SAP Task Center Mobile Start setup has been completed. The configuration was done through
the Launchpad Service by the site administrator.

A new Group and Role have been added.

An X.509 certificate has been applied and the configuration has been updated in the Cloud
Connector section.
The service settings for Task Center have been configured in the S/4HANA system.
The Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS URL has been configured in the BTP subaccount.

A destination has been created in the BTP Destination section to connect to S/4HANA.

At the end, we couldn't see any item on Task Center list.

entry.title: Curious to know if SAP is selling fresh licences of SAP ECC, Greenfield Implementations?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Curious to know if SAP is still selling the fresh licences of SAP ECC system, for Greenfield Implementations? Ideally it is sensible to opt for S4 system, if we are implementing it afresh. but, is SAP ECC system available, if I want to opt for it.?

In Specific, would like to understand, till which year, SAP ECC system is available for purchase, in case of new Greenfield implementations.! 

THIS QUESTION IS NOT ABOUT THE SUPPORT AVAILABILITY FOR SAP ECC, This is in the context of fresh implementation.

Thanks & Regards,
Satya

entry.title: Re: The 15 Most Important Topics for the ABAP Developer of the Future!
entry.content: Very impressive and comprehensive insights, I'd say ABAP is evolving too much nowadays, many of the Devs still stay in your comfort zone before 90s (the ABAP OO coming out).

entry.title: Sourcing & Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Hello procurement folks,

please welcome SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Release 2502. In the fast-paced world of business, a smooth procurement process can make all the difference. These days, that efficiency comes down to having the right software. In this post, we'll take a look at the latest procurement updates and explore how these advancements can simplify your operations, cut costs, and ultimately boost your bottom line. Watch the video and find the top highlights of this release.

For other releases please see here.

Operational Procurement

  • Punchout Capability for Supplier hosted Catalogs
  • Enabling partner functions in the Purchase Order API

SAP Ariba Central Procurement

  • Mass Change of Price Scales in Central Purchase Contracts
  • Support of Enhanced Limit in Central Supplier Confirmation
  • Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement (6BJ)

Invoice Management

  • Alternative Reconciliation Account
  • My Inbox: CDS Based Custom Attributes for Supplier Invoices

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

  • Use of Document Information Extraction templates
  • Supporting different start and step conditions (amount, PO-based, credit memo)
  • Automatic reprocessing of invoices with missing receipts

Operational Procurement

Punchout Capability for Supplier hosted Catalogs

The base edition of SAP Ariba Buying delivers a smart and simple purchasing experience designed to make it easier for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers to jumpstart their path to best-in-class procurement performance, paving the way for broader digital transformation whenever your business is ready.

SAP Ariba Buying creates a simple, smart, and elegant purchasing experience that increases user engagement for indirect material procurement.  The newly added punch-out functionality enables buyers to directly access and purchase goods/services from catalog's maintained and hosted by the suppliers. This allows buyers to quickly add items from external stores to their shopping cart and initiate a purchase request that follows the approval process configured. Access real-time information on pricing and availability from external stores during the buying process. 

You wonder how this is being set up? The punchout catalogs are configured in the configuration section of SAP Ariba Buying, Base Edition via Manage Shopping Sites , here you have to configure every details of the punchout catalog like authentication, URL etc. For more information, see: Adding a Supplier Store

Enabling partner functions in the Purchase Order API

Partner functions enable to manage the relationships of different suppliers. Certain partner functions be maintained in the business partner (supplier), which can be copied to the procurement documents under each respective Purchasing Organization. With this innovation, the Purchase Order OData V4 API can be used to send the partner function fields. Use the OData V4 API 'CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001' to create, update, read, and delete partner functions in the purchase order header. For more information, see: Purchase Order (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) and Purchase Order (OData V4).

back to top

SAP Ariba Central Procurement

Mass Change of Price Scales in Central Purchase Contracts

We increase the flexibility and usability for mass managing of central purchase contracts. This innovation is an extension of the existing mass change capability for central purchase contracts through offline Microsoft Excel upload. SAP Ariba Central Procurement now also supports the update and creation of price scale values for condition types configured for price scale maintenance. Furthermore, purchasers can now choose to only upload changes in preparation mode and releasing those through a later mass update. Support for additional fields by the offline Excel upload including:

  • Numerator and denominator on item level
  • Planned delivery duration on item level
  • Item category on item level
  • Unit of measurement conversion on price-condition level
  • Incoterms classification and location 1 on distribution level 

For more information, see: Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts

Support of Enhanced Limit in Central Supplier Confirmation

With this release, you can now create a central supplier confirmation for a centrally managed purchase order with E (Enhanced Limit) in the 'Manage Central Supplier Confirmations' app. You can add, view and change confirmation items of item categories Standard (blank), lean services and E (Enhanced Limit).  The Enhanced Limit is an item category in a purchase order that can be used to order unplanned services and that is defined by a maximum amount.

Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement (6BJ)

We are happy to speed up procurement processes with the integration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Business Network through SAP Integration Suite. Central purchasers can now create central purchase orders in the central procurement hub system by pooling purchase orders from connected systems, such as SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA or ECC. The purchase orders are sent to SAP Business Network and the required suppliers are notified. Suppliers submit their order confirmation through SAP Business Network. The supplier's order confirmation is sent from SAP Business Network to either hub or connected system. As soon as the supplier starts the release and approval process for a purchase order, the status is updated in the connected system. For more information, see: 'Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement' and 'Setting Up Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement' and SAP Knowledge Base Article here.

back to top

Invoice Management

Alternative Reconciliation Account

Accounts payable accountants can now enter predefined values into the G/L account field on the details screen of the Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced app. The same applies to the reconciliation account field under general information in the Manage Supplier Invoices app. In the Import Supplier Invoices app, the downloaded template now includes the field reconciliation account. Upfront, the configuration expert needs to define the alternative reconciliation accounts allowed for each reconciliation account via SSCUI 'Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts' (SSCUI ID 102631). For more information, see: Alternative Reconciliation

 

My Inbox: CDS Based Custom Attributes for Supplier Invoices

Your manager needs more information during his daily workflow tasks: As a configuration expert, you can now include custom attributes from the CDS entity I_SupplierInvoiceAPI01 in the expert view in the My Inbox app. Check out the configuration interface 'Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes' (SSCUI ID 106664). For more information, see: My Inbox and My Inbox Expert View 

back to top

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Use of Document Information Extraction templates

Document Information Extraction service integrated with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management is an OCR based solution (ML) that enables to automatically extract data from an incoming supplier invoice (PDF or image). The use of Document Information Extraction templates allows users to extract information from invoices, based on the specific layout used by each supplier. These templates help the machine learning model to better identify and extract data fields from invoices that were initially missed or incorrectly processed. This leads to more accurate and efficient data extraction.

When uploading an invoice in the inbound flow of Central Invoice Management, a field on the invoice is not correctly detected. The power user then fixes the extraction results in the document and assigns it to a template. This trains the machine learning model to recognize the correct layout. Once a new invoice - with the same complex layout - is uploaded, the information will be extracted with higher confidence. Now, without any manual adjustments, the invoice can be posted in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This significantly streamlines the process and reduces manual intervention, making invoice management more efficient and reliable. 

Document Information Extraction Templates app is available without any activation required. However, users must be granted with some new authorizations to be able to see and use it. For more information, see:Use Document Information Extraction Templates

Supporting different start and step conditions (amount, PO-based, credit memo)

We increase flexibility by introducing more conditions to initiate the approval workflow because different invoices require different customer-specific approval. We enable customers to define the best approval process for each situation by enabling the common information model (CIM) to deliver several approval criteria that could be used as part of start and step conditions: 

  • Invoice is based on Purchase Order
  • Invoice contains at least one non-purchase order item
  • Supplier invoice type is credit memo
  • Gross Amount in company code currency

For more information, see New Start and Step Conditions for Approval Workflow

Automatic reprocessing of invoices with missing receipts

You can now activate automatic reprocessing of invoices with missing receipts within the 'Manage Processing Services' app. When you enable this process, you can determine the number of reprocessing cycles for invoices with missing receipts. This feature allows for better visibility of invoice statuses and reduces the number of invoices which require manual invoice processing. During the Reference Object Matching process step, the system checks for any missing receipts, either goods receipts or service entry sheets. If no receipt is posted for a purchase order item marked for goods receipt-based invoice verification, the receipt is recognized as missing. The system then moves to the new process step, awaiting receipts. For more information, see : Automatic Reprocessing of Invoices with Missing Receipts

Looking for more innovations on SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management? Check this out: 2502 

back to top

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn 🙂

More innovation can be found in the What's new viewer

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

🚀The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

😎Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.

🔎 Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:   

 

  

 

Link: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset 

back to top

entry.title: Re: Not able to login SAP
entry.content: okay , thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP HR LSMW
entry.content: Dear raymond_giuseppi thanks a lot for your reply, we tried with LSMW cause it's simplest method. I have run it using mode Display Error Only, but it does not show warning message, cause its' not error. Maybe because of this 'note that displaying a warning could stop execution, as if an error message had been raised, for some transactions) it doesn't show warning messages. Thanks a lot again, we'll try other method instead of LSMW.

entry.title: Re: Whats New for Planning Area Optimization Within SAP Analytics Cloud QRC1 2025
entry.content:
Fantastic to see that data actions can extend the Planning Area on real fact record changes, allowing a very granular scope (e.g. CustomerId & ProductId dimensions in your example).
The Table widget does not do it that way (unfortunately).

entry.title: Re: Removing leading zeroes using XPATH in Content Modifier
entry.content:
Hi @robertot4s,

It seems to work, but not too accurate. when I tried this value 

00012345000000 it was converted to 2147483647. 😂

entry.title: Re: Asset Management, Corrective Maintenance Order Type
entry.content: Hello Raheel, I tried using the Create Maintenance Order app, but it still requires me to select one of the predefined order types (YA01 to YA05). Should I make adjustments in CBC, or is there another way to resolve this? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: OData Exploration with EPM Tutorial - ES5
entry.content:
Hello MH_K,

your screenshot shows the Fiori Launchpad (URL /sap/bc/ui2/flp) and not the WEBGUI (URL /sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui). The login mask for the system of the tutorial (https://sapes5.sapdevcenter.com/sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui) for me looks like this:

After logging in to the WEBGUI interface it should look something like this showing you the SAP Easy Access Menu screen:

From there the tutorials on how to show the OK code field from the other answers should take you to your goal.

Best regards

Simon

entry.title: Re: EML: Read All Instances of a BO
entry.content:
@selectfrom 

How about selecting directly from the underlying CDS view (zstore)?

One note: Draft entities cannot be retrieved using above method; therefore, if draft data is required, you need to retrieve them from the draft table.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP Customer Experience - Q1 2025 Update
entry.content:
Welcome to 2025! As we embark on this new year filled with innovations, our team is thrilled to share the first update of the year for the Release Navigator for SAP Customer Experience. The content curated in this tool is intended to help you stay informed about the latest enhancements in your products from the Customer Experience portfolio. Quarterly updates will be provided to explore new features.

In this blog post, you can read about the update plans for the 2025 year and also which release content changes have been made in Q1 for product management teams.

Like 2024, we will continue to deliver content updates for the Release Navigator for SAP Customer Experience on a quarterly basis before product releases in February, May, August, and November. This regular schedule ensures that you will consistently receive the latest updates and content, which are important for your smooth run projects.

Each quarterly update is maintained for products that follow the harmonized release schedule: SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Marketing Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, and SAP Emarsys. You can find the specific product release dates featured on the Product-Specific page within the release navigator. Moreover, the only UX update for 2025 is planned for August. This update is focused on making your interaction with our platform even more intuitive and seamless, thereby streamlining your workflow and boosting overall efficiency.

Disclaimer: These dates are informational purposes only and may change. The Readiness@Scale team has the final decision over any UX modifications.

For SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud, there will be a new update format. Instead of traditional release webcasts, a blog post will be published in advance, with detailed written documentation on current enhancements and improvements. This will allow you to access information at your own pace, ensuring flexibility and convenience. The release content for SAP Marketing Cloud will slightly change moving forward. The team will continue to host release webcasts, offering opportunities to engage with experts and learn about new features. However, they will not create a blog post for the upcoming releases. SAP Emarsys offers a release webcast series throughout the entire 2025 year, and you can find the link on the product page. Additionally, you will always find the URL for the current release webcast there for quicker access.

In addition to the specific content changes for 2502 in the release navigator, for products that follow a specific release schedule, resources are also available for products operating on a continuous delivery model. These include

  1. SAP Sales Version 2
  2. Service Cloud Version 2
  3. SAP Commerce Cloud.

Recap information for these products is provided after each quarter. More specific information is always available on the respective product pages.

As we continuously enhance the release navigator for you, we plan to add new content to the SAP Commerce Cloud product page in the near future. Stay tuned for updates.

With the help of badges introduced last year, you can easily identify new content, content that is about to be retired, or content of high importance on the release navigator.

Badges

The Readiness@Scale is a cross-solution team responsible for release readiness topics in the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Access all the release navigators at Release Ready page and find our team blogs with the tag Readiness@Scale.

If you have any feedback or specific requests regarding the release navigator, we would be more than happy to hear from you. We appreciate all feedback and value your input.

Readiness@Scale with SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Re: EML: Read All Instances of a BO
entry.content: Thank you for the answer. This is a work-around that would probably work but not quite what I'm looking for. Wouldn't this circumvent the BO logic by going directly to the data? That doesn't seem like an optimal solution to me

entry.title: SAP Roadmap available in CALM but not in Roadmap Viewer?
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am doing a PoC for a Brownfield Conversion Project to S/4HANA and choosed in the project set-up the Roadmap "RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - System Conversion". Unfortunately I can´t find this roadmap in the Roadmap Viewer for getting a deeper look. Do someone know how this roadmap is named in the Roadmap viewer?

Thanks in advance

Karola Pirkl

entry.title: Re: cloud connector alerts not receiving via email
entry.content: I don't think the Cloud Connector has any influence on this. Check the error log of your mail server. Perhaps the user/sender address does not have permission to send to some of the receivers?!

entry.title: Re: SPOT awards - Award history
entry.content: Hi @Ravi14 did you manage to find a solution to this as we are trying to do the same thing?

entry.title: Using a Lag key figure in the calculation of another key figure
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a KF where the future periods show a forecasted value and the historical periods show a forecast that is stored in lag key figure.  I have not been able to determine if it is possible (and if so how) to refer to a specific Lag in the calculation.  

Specifically I want to pull Lag 2 for the past periods.  I know how to handle the future/past split (as I have several key figures working this way) but I am struggling to pull the lag value (or any to put any attribute specific condition) into the calculation spec,

Thanks,

Jez

entry.title: Re: Open Items Display in FBL1N but F-44 is not taking th...
entry.content: Have you selected special G/L accounts in FBL1N, but not during clearing?

entry.title: Re: How can I build a sum over an associated table in SAP...
entry.content: Hi, I resolved the issue. Actually it works fine, since the join is basically grouping the receipts per trip. However, I forgot to deploy the changes to my database, so I got this error

entry.title: Re: Monitoring for SAP B1 HANA
entry.content: HI Ankit. Cockpit and Solution Manager require additional resources in terms of now having to add additional space or resources ontop of what a customer has purchased regarding HANA. As an example. Cockpit requires an additinal 32GB or ram to run as per the requirements and adding these resources to a customers server has significant cost impliciations. Also to add that Solution Manager in my opnion does not give clear procedure into installation and setup. Hence the reason i am asking is there a 3rd party tool that can be integrated into a HANA enviroment for SAP B1 that enables the end customer to view a dashboard and see the metircs needed for troubleshooting and alerts. If there are no tools that you are aware or anyone else then i will continue with my search. WOuld be interesting to know what other SAP partners are using besides Cockpit and SM

entry.title: How to Achieve a Side-by-Side Layout in a Fiori List Report While Keeping Variants?
entry.content:
Context:

I’m working on an SAP Fiori List Report application and need to achieve a side-by-side layout where:
✅A Smart Table occupies 75% of the width
✅A Pie Chart (Smart Chart) occupies 25% of the width
✅The layout must be fully responsive
✅Variant Management should be preserved

Approaches I Have Tried:

1️⃣Standard Fiori Elements List Report:

  • I attempted to use annotations in CDS to define both the table and chart.
  • However, I couldn’t control the layout (side-by-side) without custom CSS, which I want to avoid.

2️⃣Fiori Elements Extensions (Custom View in List Report):

  • I created a custom view extension and added a table and chart inside an HBox.
  • The layout works, but Variant Management is lost because it's outside the standard Smart Table controls.

3️⃣Analytical List Page (ALP) instead of List Report:

  • ALP supports side-by-side visuals by default.
  • However, I need List Report features (filters, actions, navigation) rather than ALP.

Question:

🔹Is it possible to achieve this side-by-side layout in a List Report while keeping variant management?
🔹If not, should I switch to a Custom Page and manually implement variants?
🔹Any best practices for keeping the UI responsive and aligned with Fiori guidelines?

 

Thank you in advanced for the help.

entry.title: Re: HTML5 Application 403 Forbidden error while calling Cloud Connector
entry.content: Any luck? i am also facing the same issue

entry.title: Re: Curious to know if SAP is selling fresh licences of S...
entry.content:
Hello @Satya58 

I don't think fresh ECC licenses are available for new implementations. The same way as fresh Ford Ts are not available anymore 😉

ECC licenses might still be there in case e.g. when you need to buy more named users for an existing installation.

Go with S/4HANA for any new project. However you might still find it difficult to get on premise licenses as SAP is pushing cloud products.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Problem whit ATP check in production
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

My company is making the transition to S4H (rise), we are in the test phase and the following problem is occurring during ATP in production order whit subcontracting operation.

Scenario: Subcontracting phase in production. During the production process the semi-finished product is sent to the subcontractor for processing. To avoid problems with the stocks that were lost in SAP ECC we have inserted an operation that allows the semi-finished product to be put in stock (531). This is then picked up and sent to external processing.

Now we have a problem with ATP that does not confirm the quantity of the semi-finished product, preventing the release of the order.

In SAP ECC. instead the 531 (negative quantity in BOM) is seen as an entry (entry to stock) and therefore the quantity of the requirement for the same material is confirmed in the next position.

Has anything changed from this point of view? I have verified that the MRP areas have no impact.
Attached is an example and the ATP rules for open and released production order.

Thanks for the help
Best regards
Giuseppe

entry.title: Re: Invoice pricing and services date equal to sy-datum
entry.content: Set routine 11 against the field "Data VBRK/VBRP in VTFL

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Flush method is sap mdg fpm
entry.content: Hi, Sure. Thanks for suggestions.

entry.title: Re: Building Robust Enterprise Architecture: Unlocking Value Streams (In Business Context)
entry.content:
@yuyao_qi , Thanks for connecting. To identify processes within a value stream based on enterprise architecture (EA), you’ll need to connect the dots between the business goals, processes, information systems, and technology infrastructure. The goal is to map out how value is created, delivered, and captured from the perspective of both the business and its underlying systems.

For the above Online Retail Store example, the value stream is essentially the series of activities that create and deliver value to the customer, from the moment they first engage with your store until after they’ve received their purchase and possibly interacted with customer service. We take only customer order fulfilment (as value stream) from eg. inventory management, the processes involved are order picking, packing, and shipment labeling (This ensures the right product is available and shipped efficiently which is highest value), systems inside are Inventory management system, warehouse management system, shipping software.

Similarly, you can do another value stream Customer Feedback & Repeat Purchase (this ensures customer service responds quickly and effectively). 

By analyzing each part of the value stream, we can ensure that our online retail store is delivering value in the most effective and customer-centric way possible.

Hope it helps.

Thanks and Best regards,

Jansi

entry.title: currency issue
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

am going to activate of material ledger , but status says ML currency type but not FI Currency type how to solve it although am using S4HANA. 

can anyone please provide any solution ?

entry.title: Setting Status of Networks BAPI_BUS2002_SET_STATUS
entry.content:
Hi. I am currently setting status of Networks using BAPI_BUS2002_SET_STATUS via ProcessRunner. However, I am encountering error: E:CNIF_STATUS-005: Error in processing. Function was not executed.

I am using these BAPIs: 
BAPI_PS_INITIALIZATION
BAPI_BUS2002_SET_STATUS
BAPI_PS_PRECOMMIT
BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting - New Balance Validation Process
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, we will discuss about the new Balance Validation process that is delivered with S/4HANA CE2502. The following Fiori apps are part of this release:

  • Run Balance Validation and View Results
  • Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups
  • Assign Rule Group for Balance Validation

In the new Balance Validation process, you can also create a new task by using the app "Define Balance Validation Task" under Manage Your Solution (F1241) > Configure Your Solution > Application Area: Finance Sub Application Area: Corporate Close > Data Processing.

Additionally, you can import or export the rule group assignments using the following Fiori app:

  • Import/Export Validation Settings

Transition Process

The toggle 'FINCS_BV_ADAPTATION' should be switched-on to view the above apps and also to activate this new Balance Validation process. Please use the app Manage Your Solution (F1241) > Configure Your Solution > Application Area: Finance Sub Application Area: Corporate Close > Data Processing to switch on the toggle. Please follow the below note for the migration process:

  • 3553668 - Group Reporting: Migration of new Balance Validation Process

The steps of the migration is explained with screenshots in the attached file of the above note.  

Is it possible to create a new Task?

Yes. You can refer to the below video that explains the creation of Balance Validation task.

As you can notice, apart from Cons. Unit and Cons. Group categories you can define a new task based on accounting. Here are the  details about Standard tasks that are delivered through content:

  • S0080 - Balance Validation in Accounting
  • S1080 - Balance Validation at Consolidation Unit Level (Reported Data)
  • S1180 - Balance Validation at Consolidation Unit Level (Standardized Data)
  • S2980 - Balance Validation at Consolidation Group Level (Consolidated Data)

How to create a Rule Group?

The rules can be defined using the app "Define Validation Rules" and those rules can be added in the Rule Group as shown below.

You can Navigate to "Define Validation Rules" from this app as shown in the below screenshot.

 

Rule Group Assignment

It is mandatory to assign the Tasks to the Rule Group. The existing Validation Methods are migrated to the rule group as explained in the SNOTE "3553668 - Group Reporting: Migration of new Balance Validation Process". You can refer to the below video that explains the Task assignment to Rule Group:

What are the steps to attach the Task to Monitor(s)?

The tasks that are created using the "Define Balance Validation Task" app can be attached to the Monitor as shown below.

It is also possible to execute the Tasks  in the app "Run Balance Validation and View Results" as shown in the below screenshot.

How is the new Solution better than the old one?

The new Balance Validation process has the following benefits:

  • You can define a new task by choosing one of the Task Categories and run the rule definitions either in Group Reporting or in Accounting as a data source.
  • The performance is optimized and the memory consumption has relatively improved
  • It is now possible to define a maximum of 6 Group By fields
  • All Balance Validation tasks can be executed in one app: Run Balance Validation and View Results. 

Concluding with Additional Links

Please feel free to share your thoughts by commenting on this blog. Also, we can use this blog as a communication tool for any clarification that you require on this topic. You can also follow my profile for more content on analytical application and Balance Validation for group reporting. I would recommend posting your questions by specifying the tag: “SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting”

Define Balance Validation Tasks | SAP Help Portal

Balance Validation Tasks and Task Categories | SAP Help Portal

Working with Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups | SAP Help Portal

Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups | SAP Help Portal

Assign Rule Groups for Balance Validation | SAP Help Portal

Run Balance Validation and View Results | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Suppress Custom uibb in sap mdg Customer
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have created the custom uibb in sap mdg for customer few months before and now we want to remove the block from the UI.

We have removed the block from the UI via configuration.

Doubt is, Is there an option to suppress the block completely so that when we create the Change request we observed that all the methods like get definition/flush/get_data/process event been called.

We want to suppress so that all these method of standard code will not run as it will be better in terms of performance. Thanks.

Kindly suggest me the same so that i dont want the custom entity to be called when the CR is created. 

Regards,

Karthik S

entry.title: Request for Lease Management Solution in standard SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

Please provide a solution for lease management in SAP B1. Below are the details:

We have multiple locations, all of which are on rent. We require a lease management system in SAP B1 to efficiently manage our rental agreements.

Kindly guide us on how to implement lease management in SAP B1.

Looking forward to your response.

entry.title: Performance testing
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

One of the project team is conducting performance testing on the Prod environment and when they simulated a large number of users say 100 + at the same time, they have come across the error - "Unable to connect to OIDC provider". Also, when we checked on accounts.login event, we could see "Api rate limit exceeded" error.

They did in stages and 100 users were ramped up in 5 minutes form, so at least 10+ users per seconds as an approximation on requests hit

Can you help me with the resolution for this please. 

 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Performance testing
entry.content:
Hi Team,

The rate limit system is set to support a low volume of traffic from a high number of users/devices (IP-s) and not a high volume of traffic from a low number of users/devices (IP-s).

To avoid hitting rate limit please make sure that only a small amount of accounts.login call made from the same IP address, e.g. couple of hundred calls/hour/IP, to avoid triggering rate limit.

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Re: SAP BW/4HANA: The Grey Panther of Data Warehousing

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Possible to change the item category when copying the...
entry.content:
I could like to confirm you that its the standard behavior that when line item is copied for framework PO to create new line, the item category and account assignment category will be copied and can't be edited. Its the standard design.

I have double checked in our internal system as well and confirming you here.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Business Rule to send a specific Checklist to an emai...
entry.content:
Hi Brice,
We do have a few Business Rule Actions that might be helpful for your scenario.  They are:

  • Build Checklist Report
  • Build Report
  • Send Email with Attachment

You can find more details regarding these actions here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/actions.html?locale=en-US

If you need more information or have additional questions, please let me know.  Kind Regards, Wendy

entry.title: Re: Removing leading zeroes using XPATH in Content Modifier
entry.content:
Hi @ZTK196 ,

Too long to convert to number 😞

Then, try with a pattern for string: replace(//number, '^0+', '')

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Business processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Central Business Configuration learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will enable you to configure business processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Central Business Configuration.

Learning objectives

  • Set up the implementation projects for the development system tenants
  • Define the scope and country/region localizations
  • Select the primary finance settings and build the organizational structure
  • Define and transport product-specific configuration values
  • Create users and grant access to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Development System tenants
  • Create users and grant access to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test and Production Systems

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Central Business Configuration   

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: How to Monitor the Impact of Assigning a Consignment Business Partner to a Warehouse in SAP Business
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I've configured a warehouse in SAP Business One and assigned a Business Partner (BP) as the Consignment Business Partner. I'm seeking guidance on how to observe and analyze the effects of this assignment within my documents and reports. Specifically:

  • Which documents or transactions reflect the association between the warehouse and the consignment BP?
  • Are there particular reports that highlight inventory levels, movements, or financial impacts related to consignment stock?
  • How can I ensure that the consignment stock is accurately tracked and reported in the system?
  • Does selecting a Consignment Business Partner for a warehouse automatically populate any fields in related transactions or documents?

Any insights or recommendations on effectively monitoring and managing consignment stock with this setup would be greatly appreciated.

and is the business partner field related to consignment business partner? if yes how does it work ?

Thank you!

entry.title: Datasphere Replication Flow logs
entry.content:
I want to be able to check on the status of my replication flows by checking the log/database tables so I can monitor and check on replication flow execution status programatically.  When I look at the Data Integration Monitor within Datasphere, I can see the Run log details and the Object log details displayed.

The Run log details can be found in the monitoring tables ("TASK_LOGS_V_EXT" and "TASK_LOG_MESSAGES_V_EXT") but I cannot find the Object log details.  See attached file for the Object log details that I am looking for

Does anyone know if these are available?

regs

entry.title: Re: Large Backup Catalog Size and Process to Maintain it
entry.content:
Hello Sheo,

We generated the command based on 36 days retention policy and it ran successfully. However, we the oldest backup showing is now 253 days which was earlier 1000 days. I suspect that the command only covered full backups and not differential backups. Hence last 36 weeks full backups are available in backup catalog.

Our backup policy is weekly full back followed by daily differential backups.

Is there any update we can use in the command.

Thanks

Deepak Sharma

entry.title: Re: Why "Just Ask" feature not available for Neo tenant?
entry.content:
Hi Vijai,
That is correct SAP Analytics Cloud just ask NLQ feature is not available on Neo tenants nor on Alibaba Cloud tenants.
SAP Analytics Cloud investment and new features are focussed on Google Cloud,  Microsoft Azure and Amazon AWS infrastructures. 
There is no plan to release just ask feature on SAP Analytics Cloud tenants running on Neo
Thanks,
François

entry.title: Re: SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition: functional comparison
entry.content: Can I use SAP EWM for S/4HANA on premise in combination with S/4HANA Cloud, private cloud edition and what are the Framework conditions?

entry.title: Re: How To Read Data From TCP/IP Socket and Port Using AB...
entry.content:
To my knowledge, the ABAP programming language does not provide the tools for writing a TCP/IP client. (There is the ABAP Push Channel framework, but this provides only TCP/IP server functionality. However, you need client functionality here.)

The best way I can see, is to use a middleware that communicates with the ABAP side via RFC protocol, and with the program on the remote host via TCP/IP protocol. For example, you could use SAP Business Connector, which provides all the RFC connectivity out of the box, and allows custom Java or C/C++ code to be plugged in, and you could then write the communication code with the remote host using standard java.net.Socket.

You should also check: often third-party products don't require you to write low-level networking code yourself, but provide some kind of "client library", that does it for you. If this is the case here as well, e.g. a client library is available for Java or for C/C++, then you could install that library on the Business Connector and use it easily.

Steps to do would be:

  1. Download and install SAP BC. (Takes 5min. No extra license fee is required, if you have a standard SAP license, but a one-time registration is needed to get to the download page.)
  2. On ABAP side, create a destination of type "T" in SM59.
  3. On SAP BC side, configure logon data for the SAP system and a matching "Listener" to the above SM59-destination. (Configuration)
  4. On SAP BC side, write a few Java or C/C++ Services that do the communication with 192.168.0.1:1702.
    (E.g. one each for connection open, read data, write data, connection close. Or if the communication is a simple "one-step request", you could put the entire code into one single Service, which returns the final output, and then call this from ABAP.)
  5. On ABAP side, create empty function modules with input/output, who match the above "Services".
  6. On SAP BC side, create Inbound Maps, where you bind the function modules to the Services. (Configuration)
  7. In your ABAP code, call the function modules on the destination pointing to the SAP BC. Voilà.

Here some documentation: Documentation
You will need the SAPAdaperGuide for the necessary configuration steps, and the DeveloperGuide for how to write Java or C/C++ Services.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere News in December
entry.content:
Hello,

Folders are supported now. But how do I get the information on folders with the API? Which objects are in which folders? What is their hierarchical structure? And how can I create folders with the API? 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/d0ecd6f297ac40249072a44df0549c1a/6f5c65f209004751aa48f9682ee2ec45.html

entry.title: Re: how to create two outbound cremas idoc if same vendor is extended to two company codes
entry.content:
I have added the ALE tag. It's most probably a question for the ALE experts.

If ALE doesn't provide such a feature, a workaround would be to send the IDoc to a middleware tool (e.g. SAP BC), and here set it up so that it sends the IDoc to two receivers.

(However, that opens up a whole can of worms... What to do in cases, where the IDoc is sent to the first receiver successfully, but the second receiver fails due to a network error...? If you reply "OK" to the SAP system, the second receiver will never receive the IDoc, and if you reply with "Rolled Back", then the SAP system will repeat the IDoc, and the first receiver may receive it twice... You will probably have to setup "store & forward" and separate status keeping for both receivers on the middleware.)

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - SAP Community Escape House - UI5

entry.title: Does SAP provide a solution to improve employee onboarding, retention, and development?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering SAP Solutions for Talent Management learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is designed to help you discover the SAP Talent Management solution area. You will equip yourself with the skills to articulate the value of SAP Talent Management. 

Learning Objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, you will be able to leverage the potential of the SAP Talent Management solution, build a comprehensive vision for its implementation, and extend its value across various applications. 

Prerequisites

  • Discovering the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Portfolio

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Polish Localization

entry.title: Re: Polish Localization

entry.title: Force Confirmation
entry.content:
Why Force Confirmed Sales Orders are not included in Confirmation Update Runs ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Force Confirmation
entry.content:
Hi All,

  • The Confirmation Update Runs subview allows you to manage mass data runs for availability checks and updating of sales orders, service orders, and stock transfer orders. It verifies the confirmed dates and quantities for sales orders items to ensure they are still valid or need to be updated.
  • However when manually Force Confirmation is done it states that it is important and no further check is required to update as it has already reached for execution for next step as it is confirmed already.
  • You can reset a forced confirmation by canceling the confirmation or by checking the availability for the customer demand again. Note that forced confirmations are not included in confirmation update runs.
  • Refer help center document :Availability Checks | SAP Help Portal 

Best Regards,
Kopal Upadhyay
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: Consuming SAP Build API's made easy with SAP Cloud SDK in your CAP application
entry.content: Thanks for the guide. In your Architecture diagram i see that you have drawn the picture of the Mobile also! does that mean now that you can have a native mobile app(java/kotlin or swift) to connect with AppRouter?
